questions.q: Banda Singh Bahadur is best described as which kind of leader?
questions.explanation: He was a Sikh military commander who led campaigns in the Punjab.

questions.q: In which century did Banda Singh Bahadur lead his campaigns?
questions.explanation: He led campaigns in the early 18th century CE.

questions.q: Banda Singh Bahadur fought against the authorities of which empire?
questions.explanation: He led campaigns against the Mughal authorities in the Punjab.

questions.q: Banda Singh Bahadur began his campaigns after the death of which Sikh leader?
questions.explanation: He took up arms after the death of Guru Gobind Singh.

questions.q: In which region did Banda Singh Bahadur establish Sikh rule?
questions.explanation: He established Sikh rule in parts of the Punjab.

questions.q: What happened to Banda Singh Bahadur in 1716?
questions.explanation: He was captured and executed by the Mughals in 1716.

questions.q: Banda Singh Bahadur is often remembered as the first Sikh what?
questions.explanation: He is remembered as the first Sikh sovereign to establish a state in the Punjab.

questions.q: Banda Singh Bahadur belonged to which religious community?
questions.explanation: He was a Sikh commander carrying forward the cause of the Khalsa.

questions.q: Banda Singh Bahadur's struggle was primarily a fight for what?
options: Sikh political power and justice in the Punjab
questions.explanation: He fought to establish Sikh political power and challenge oppression in the Punjab.

questions.q: In which year was Banda Singh Bahadur executed?
questions.explanation: Banda Singh Bahadur was executed in 1716.

questions.q: Who commissioned Banda Singh Bahadur to lead the struggle in the Punjab?
questions.explanation: Guru Gobind Singh sent him north to the Punjab to lead the Sikhs against Mughal rule.

questions.q: What was Banda Singh Bahadur's name before he joined the Sikh cause as an ascetic?
questions.explanation: He was originally an ascetic known as Lachman Dev, later Madho Das Bairagi.

questions.q: Which town, scene of his sons' deaths, did Banda Singh Bahadur famously capture and avenge in 1710?
questions.explanation: He captured Sirhind in 1710, avenging the killing of Guru Gobind Singh's younger sons there.

questions.q: At which 1710 battle did Banda Singh Bahadur defeat the Mughal governor of Sirhind?
questions.explanation: He defeated Wazir Khan at the Battle of Chappar Chiri in 1710.

questions.q: Which Mughal governor, responsible for the death of the Guru's sons, was killed by Banda's forces?
questions.explanation: Wazir Khan, the governor of Sirhind, was killed at Chappar Chiri in 1710.

questions.q: Which Mughal emperor's reign saw most of Banda Singh Bahadur's campaigns?
questions.explanation: His campaigns spanned the reigns of Bahadur Shah I and Farrukhsiyar after Aurangzeb's death.

questions.q: Banda Singh Bahadur struck coins in the name of whom to assert Sikh sovereignty?
options: The Sikh Gurus (Nanak and Gobind Singh)
questions.explanation: He issued coins in the name of Guru Nanak and Guru Gobind Singh, not himself.

questions.q: Banda Singh Bahadur introduced a new seal and official greeting for his administration. What reform did he enact for peasants?
options: Abolishing the zamindari system in his territory
questions.explanation: He abolished the oppressive zamindari system, granting land rights to the tillers.

questions.q: Which fort did Banda Singh Bahadur make an early base for his administration?
questions.explanation: He fortified Lohgarh (formerly Mukhlisgarh) as a capital and base.

questions.q: Where was Banda Singh Bahadur finally besieged and captured in 1715?
questions.explanation: He was besieged and captured at Gurdas Nangal in 1715 after a long siege.

questions.q: In which city was Banda Singh Bahadur executed in 1716?
questions.explanation: He was taken to Delhi and executed there in 1716.

questions.q: Banda Singh Bahadur first met Guru Gobind Singh at which southern town?
questions.explanation: He met Guru Gobind Singh at Nanded, where the Guru transformed him into a Sikh warrior.

questions.q: What title or epithet did the Sikh tradition give Banda for his courage, reflected in his name?
questions.explanation: He was given the title Bahadur, meaning the brave, by Guru Gobind Singh.

questions.q: Banda Singh Bahadur's official seal proclaimed the rule of the Khalsa using which combined term for his polity?
options: The deg, tegh, and fateh (cauldron, sword, victory)
questions.explanation: His seal invoked deg (charity), tegh (the sword), and fateh (victory), a Khalsa motto.

questions.q: Banda Singh Bahadur introduced a new official era or calendar dating from which event?
questions.explanation: He inaugurated a new calendar era dated from his victory and capture of Sirhind.

questions.q: Which Mughal emperor ordered the campaigns that ultimately crushed Banda Singh Bahadur's state?
questions.explanation: Emperor Farrukhsiyar directed the final campaign that captured and executed Banda.

questions.q: Banda Singh Bahadur captured which town near Karnal early in his campaign, after Samana?
questions.explanation: After sacking Samana, he advanced to take Sadhaura and other towns en route to Sirhind.

questions.q: The town of Samana, an early target of Banda's forces in 1709, was notable as the home of whom?
options: The Mughal mint masters of Delhi
The executioners of Guru Tegh Bahadur and the Guru's sons
questions.explanation: Samana was home to the executioners connected to the deaths of the Gurus, making it an early target.

questions.q: Banda Singh Bahadur's legacy is credited with first demonstrating what to the Sikhs?
options: That the Khalsa could establish sovereign rule
That alliance with the British was essential
That the Gurus' line should continue in person
questions.explanation: His brief state showed that the Khalsa could win and hold sovereign political power.

questions.q: Banda Singh Bahadur was initially an ascetic of which devotional order before becoming a Sikh warrior?
questions.explanation: He had been a Bairagi (Vaishnava) ascetic named Madho Das before joining Guru Gobind Singh.
